dashboard.pickup: Pick up where you left off
dashboard.joinCourseByCode: Join Course by Sign Up Code
dashboard.joinCourseDescription: Enter the unique course sign up code associated with the course you want to join. Not sure what the sign up code is? Ask the instructor of the course!

courses.noCoursesAdded: No courses added yet. To join a course, click "Join Course" above.
courses.createCoursePrompt: To create a course, click "Create Course" above.
courses.joinCoursePrompt: Then, use the code your instructor gave you to join their course.
courses.courseListDescription: To access a course, click the "View Modules" button for your desired course and choose from the available modules. To edit or duplicate a course, click on the options menu for the course you wish to use.
courses.createCourseDescription: Courses are spaces in which instructors can create and organize modules that customize how students can interact with the AI. For more information on editing a course, please see the
courses.createCourseDescriptionLinkText: Editing a Course section of our instructor guide
courses.noCoursesMatchSearch: No courses match your search. Try a different search term.
courses.addedToCourse: You have been added to the course.
courses.duplicateCourseDescription: Enter a name and a unique sign up code for the duplicated course. Duplicating a course will also copy over all the modules and settings within this course.

modules.noModulesAvailable: No modules are currently available to you.
modules.createModulePrompt: To create a module, go to the course in which you would like to create the module.
modules.moduleListDescription: To access a module, click the "Begin Module" button for the desired module. For information on creating, editing, copying, or viewing activity for a module, please see the
modules.createModuleDescription: Modules provide users access to conversations with the AI. Modules can be customized to allow or restrict access to specific assets, including conversation prompts (AI instructions) and documents. For more information on creating a module, please see the
modules.createModuleDescriptionLinkText: "Creating a Module" section of our instructor guide
modules.noModulesMatchSearch: No courses match your search. Try a different search term.

account.accountSettingsDescription: Manage your profile information and preferences.

about.aboutMessage:
PapyrusAI and the related instructional resources have been developed by the [Digital Learning Lab](https://www.digitallearninglab.org/) at the University of California, Irvine, in 2023 with grant funding from NSF (#23152984), UCI, The Learning Agency, and the California Education Learning Lab.

For resources on using generative AI in instruction, see our site at [GenAIed.org](https://www.genaied.org/). This tool is built on AWS serverless functions and React for the front end. We are using OpenAI's API to power our tool, but continuously evaluate other commercial models.

If you want to learn more about what we're doing or want to get involved, email us at <digitallearninglab@uci.edu>.

orgSettings.orgSettingsDescription: Manage user permissions and access levels for your organization.
orgSettings.addPermissionsDescription: Grant permission access to a user by entering their email address.
orgSettings.updatePermissionDescription: Change the permission level for this user.
orgSettings.removePermissionsDescription: This action cannot be undone. The user will lose all elevated permissions.
orgSettings.removePermissionsConfirm: Are you sure you would like to remove permissions from {{email}}? They will still have student level access.
orgSettings.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Please contact support.

common.name: Name
common.errorMessage: An error occurred. Please try again later.

components.deletePromptMessage: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this prompt?
components.deleteFileMessage: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this file?
components.deleteFolderMessage: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this folder and everything in it?
components.copyPromptToDescription: Select a folder to copy this prompt to.
components.movePromptToDescription: Select a folder to move this prompt to.
components.copyFileToDescription: Select a folder to copy this file to.
components.moveFileToDescription: Select a folder to move this file to.
components.promoteFolderDescription: Are you sure you would like to promote this folder into an organization folder along everything in it? This will remove the folder from your personal ownership and transfer it to the organization level. Proceeding will allow all instructors to be able to read and use contains in modules. All admins will be able to edit this folder.
components.demoteFolderDescription: Are you sure you would like to demote this organization folder into a personal user folder along everything in it? This will remove the folder from the organization ownership and transfer it to the user level. Proceeding will only let you edit the folder.
components.renameFolderDescription: Enter a new name for your folder.
components.thisPromptAlreadyAdded: This prompt is already added to the module
components.thisFileAlreadyAdded: This file is already added to the module
components.copyModuleToDescription: Please select a course you would like to copy this module to. Copying a module will copy over all module customizations, including the module name, description, added assets, and settings.
components.duplicateModuleDescription: Please enter a unique name for your module. Duplicating the module will also copy over all settings within this module.
components.noCoursesFoundCannotCopy: No Courses Found. Cannot copy module.
components.somethingWentWrongCreatingConversation: Something went wrong creating a new conversation. Try again later
components.somethingWentWrongLoadingConversations: Something went wrong loading conversations. Try again later
components.somethingWentWrong: Something went wrong. Try again later

createCourse.courseNameDescription: The name for your course that users will see upon joining.
createCourse.courseNamePlaceholder: e.g., English 190W: Communications in the Professional World
createCourse.courseSignUpCodeDescription: The unique sign up code that users will use to join your course. You can use any combination of letters and numbers. This is case sensitive.
createCourse.yearDescription: The year in which your course is taking place.
createCourse.termDescription: The term in which your course is taking place.
createCourse.sectionPeriodDescription: The section number or period for your course.
createCourse.teachingAssistantsDescription: The name and email address of the teaching assistant(s) assigned to your course. Teaching assistants can create and edit modules for you, but not delete or unpublish the course. You can assign multiple people to this role. In order to add someone as a teaching assistant, they must already have a PapyrusAI account.
createCourse.teachingAssistantsNote: Teaching assistants can create and edit modules for you, but not delete or unpublish the course. You can assign multiple people to this role. To add someone as a teaching assistant, they must already have a PapyrusAI account.
createCourse.savePublishDescription: To save and publish (i.e., make visible to students) your course, select "Save & Publish". If you want to save your course without publishing the course, select "Save without Publishing". Note: Choosing this option after the course has already been published will unpublish the course.
createCourse.discardChangesDescription: Are you sure you would like to discard the changes to this course? This action cannot be undone.
createCourse.deleteCourseDescription: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this course? This action cannot be undone.
createCourse.unpublishCourseDescription: This course is currently published and available to the public. Continuing will make the course unavailable to students.
createCourse.scheduleInformationDescription: Specify when this course takes place to help organize your curriculum.
createCourse.selectTeachingAssistant: Select a teaching assistant to add...
createCourse.noMoreUsersAvailable: No more users available to add
createCourse.courseDescriptionPart1: Courses are spaces in which instructors can create and organize modules that allow students to interact with the AI. For more information on creating a course, please see
createCourse.courseDescriptionLinkText: "Creating a Course" section of our instructor guide
createCourse.courseNotFoundDescription: The course you're looking for doesn't exist or has been deleted.
createCourse.getSavePublishHelp: Get help with Save & Publish options

createModule.moduleDescriptionHelptext: describe the purpose and instuctional goals for this module...
createModule.noAssetsAddedDescription: To add an asset (including prompts and documents), click "Add Asset" above.
createModule.showEmbeddedPromptDescription: Allows users to see the full text of the embedded prompt with which they begin their chat with the AI. Unchecking this will mean that the user will not be able to see the initial text of the prompt sent initially to the AI. For more information on why you might choose one or the other, see the
createModule.showEmbeddedPromptDescriptionLinkText: "Creating a Module" section of our instructor guide
createModule.allowWebSearchDescription: Allow PapyrusAI to search the internet in response to a query or question from students. Have the students prompt the AI with things like "Look up this topic", and it will get some sources from the internet, give a list of the links to the student, and read them to use those sources in conversation with the student.
createModule.selectFolderDescription: To add an asset (including prompts and documents) to your module, navigate to the folder in which the asset is hosted. Note: Assets must be created or uploaded in the Library before they can be added to a module.
createModule.savePublishDescription: To save and publish (i.e., make visible to students) your module, select "Save & Publish". If you want to save your module without publishing it, select "Save without Publishing". Note: Choosing this option after the module has already been published will unpublish the module.
createModule.discardChangesDescription: Are you sure you would like to discard the changes to this module?
createModule.deleteModuleDescription: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this module?
createModule.unpublishModuleDescription: This module is current published and available to the public. Continuing will make the module unavailable to students.
createModule.enterModuleInfo: Enter the essential details for your module
createModule.removeAssetDescription: Are you sure you would like to remove this asset from the module?
createModule.clickToAddAsset: Click here to add an asset (including prompts and documents) to the module

library.libraryDescription: The library contains all of the conversation prompts and documents hosted within PapyrusAI. By default, you have access to all prompts designed and tested by the PapyrusAI research team. You can click through the folders to browse our researcher-created prompts. If you would like to use your own assets (including your own prompts and documents) in your course, you will need to host these within your own folder. For more information on navigating the library, please see the
library.newFolderDescription: Enter a name for your personal folder, then click 'Create Folder'. Your folder and its contents will only be visible to you.
library.folderCouldNotBeCreated: Folder could not be created. Try again later.
library.tagsDescription: Tags describe a feature of the files and will be used to allow for sorting files by type.
library.manageTagsDescription: Create, edit, and delete tags for organizing content.
library.noTagsFound: No tags found. Create your first tag below.
library.tagCouldNotBeCreated: Tag could not be created. Try again later.
library.tagCouldNotBeUpdated: Tag could not be updated. Try again later.
library.addContentDescription: To create a custom prompt or upload a document, click "Add Content" above.
library.filtersDescription: Filter folders by date, tags, owner, and more.
library.folderContains: contains X prompts and Y files
library.noAssetsAdded: To add an asset (including prompts and documents) to your module, navigate to the folder in which the asset is hosted.
library.noAssetsAddedNote: Note: Assets must be created or uploaded in the Library before they can be added to a module.
library.getStarted: Get started by creating your first prompt or uploading a file.

createPrompt.createPromptDescription: Prompts function in PapyrusAI as the first set of instructions sent to the AI that will guide students' interactions with the AI. For more information on creating a prompt, please see the
createPrompt.createPromptDescriptionLinkText: "Creating a Prompt" section of our instructor guide
createPrompt.editPromptDescription: Update the prompt name and content
createPrompt.promptNameTooltip: The name for the prompt that users will see. We recommend choosing a name that makes it easy for students to understand what the prompt will do or help them with.
createPrompt.promptTooltip: The instructions that will be sent to the AI (i.e., the first message sent to the AI that will guide the interaction).
createPrompt.tagTooltip: Tags describe a feature of the prompts and will be used to allow for sorting prompts by type.
createPrompt.deletePromptMessage: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this prompt?
createPrompt.promptCouldNotBeUpdated: Prompt could not be updated. Try again later.
createPrompt.promptCouldNotBeCreated: Prompt could not be created. Try again later.
createPrompt.discardChangesDescription: Are you sure you would like to discard the changes to this prompt? This action cannot be undone.
createPrompt.enterPromptDetails: Enter the details for your prompt
createPrompt.promptInfoDescription: Prompts function in PapyrusAI as the first set of instructions sent to the AI that will guide students' interactions with the AI. When you click "Save & Publish", your prompt will be saved to your library. For more information on creating a prompt, please see the
createPrompt.promptInfoDescriptionLinkText: "Creating a Prompt" section of our instructor guide

createFile.createFileDescription: You can upload documents for your course that will factor into generated AI output. For more information on this system, please see the
createFile.createFileDescriptionLinkText: "Uploading a Document" section of our instructor guide
createFile.fileTooltip: Select a JPEG, PNG, PDF, TXT, DOCX file.
createFile.fileTooLarge: File is too large. File must be smaller than 1GB.
createFile.invalidFileType: Invalid file type. Please select a JPEG, PNG, PDF, TXT, DOCX file.
createFile.discardChangesDescription: Are you sure you would like to discard the changes to this file? This action cannot be undone.
createFile.deleteFileMessage: Are you sure you would like to permanently delete this file?
createFile.fileCouldNotBeCreated: File could not be created. Try again later.
createFile.fileInfoDescription:
You can upload documents for your course that will factor into generated AI output. When you click "Save & Upload", your file will be uploaded and saved to your library. 

For more information on this system, please see the
createFile.fileInfoDescriptionLinkText: "Uploading a Document" section of our instructor guide
createFile.editingFilesDescription: Update your file name, replace the file content, or modify tags as needed. Click "Save & Publish" to save your changes.
createFile.editingFileDescriptionNote: Note: Choosing "Discard Changes" will return you to the previous page without saving any modifications.
createFile.currentPreviewDescription: Preview of your current file content.

reports.dailyConvoLengthDescription: Daily conversation lengths chart showing average conversation length over time
reports.dailyConvoCountsDescription: Daily conversation counts chart showing number of conversations over time
reports.chatClassDescription: Chat classification chart showing count of conversations by classification type
reports.dateRangeDescription: Select a date range to view data
reports.noDataDescription: No data available for this course. This could mean
reports.noDataDescription1: No conversations have been recorded yet
reports.noDataDescription3: No students have interacted with the course
reports.moduleUsageDescription: Module usage chart showing count of conversations per module
reports.individualDailyConvoLength: Individual student daily conversation lengths chart showing average conversation length over time
reports.individualDailyConvoCount: Individual student daily conversation counts chart showing number of conversations over time
reports.individualClassification: Individual student classification counts chart showing conversation classifications
reports.individualStudentModuleUsage: Individual student module usage chart showing module usage counts
reports.reportsDescription: Reports summarize users' activity and interactions with the AI in your courses. For any course of which you are the instructor, you may view specific students' interactions with AI. For more information on reports please see the
reports.reportsDescriptionLinkText: "Instructor Reports" section of our instructor guide
reports.downloadCourseDescription:
Choose the courses you want to download and select the format.

Note: downloading multiple courses may take several minutes.
reports.courseReportsSearch: Search courses by name, instructor, section...
reports.noStudentsInClass: No students found in this class.
reports.clickViewStudentReports: Click on any student to view their individual reports
reports.stackedDailyConvoCounts: Stacked daily conversation counts chart showing number of conversations by student over time
reports.stackedDailyConvoLengths: Stacked daily conversation lengths chart showing average conversation length by student over time
reports.stackedClassificationCounts: Stacked classification counts chart showing conversation classifications by student
reports.stackedModuleUseage: Stacked module usage chart showing module usage counts by student
reports.studentReportDescription: The student report page summarizes a specific student's interactions with AI in all of the courses of which you are an instructor. To view a student's conversations within a given module, click "List Conversations" on the row for your desired module.
reports.noDataStudentDescription: No conversation data available for this student. This could mean
reports.noDataStudentDescription1: The student hasn't had any conversations yet
reports.noDataStudentDescription2: You don't have access to this student's data
reports.noDataStudentDescription3: The student hasn't been enrolled in any courses
reports.listConversationsDescription: Click "List Conversations" to view detailed interactions

chat.archiveConversationDescription: Are you sure you would like to archive this conversation? This conversation will no longer be visible in your conversation list. Instructors can still view archived conversations.
chat.renameConversationDescription: Enter a new name for this conversation.
chat.conversationListDescription: This page lists all of the conversations started within this module. For more information on viewing, renaming, and hiding conversations, please see the
chat.conversationListDescriptionLinkText: "Starting a Conversation" section of our user guide
chat.conversationListNewConvoDescription: To start a new conversation with the AI, click "New Conversation". To continue an existing conversation with the AI, click "Chat" next to the desired conversation. (If you wish to return to an existing conversation, consider renaming it to more easily identify and return to a particular conversation at a later time.)
